question: Which provider-neutral compatibility fields, tolerances, and conflict rules are sufficient for an automatic deterministic match between agent-capture and statement-row evidence?
context: Resolved from the owner-approved conservative policy on 2026-07-22. The redacted symbolic matrix at docs/validation/ledger/oq-13-reconciliation-policy.md records outcomes without retaining or inferring private statement data.
decision: ReconciliationPolicyV1 automatically matches only when approved statement-row evidence has exact owned account identity, ZAR currency, signed Int64 minor-unit amount, and Effective Date with zero-day tolerance; exactly one active recorded-but-unreconciled compatible candidate and zero guard candidates survive; and neither evidence nor candidate has a conflicting active confirmation or decision. The deterministic reason is exact_unique_candidate, the decision records the four-field basis and policy version, and replay is idempotent. Posting date, payment attribution, description, provider data, Evidence Kind, and candidate order are not compatibility fields or tie-breakers. Differing facts, shifted dates, multiple or guard candidates, conflicts, already-reconciled candidates, and stale or unsupported policy are review-required and apply no automatic financial effect. The automatic correction subset is empty. Zero-candidate statement-only creation and statement-authoritative correction remain explicit owner-approved dispositions with attributable append-only history.
